metadata.title: A Minimal Paper for Testing the IEEE Compiler
metadata.abstract: This is a minimal test paper to validate that the IEEE paper compiler correctly renders title, authors, affiliations, abstract, keywords, and a few body paragraphs into a two-column IEEE-formatted document. No figures, tables, or equations are included in this fixture.
metadata.conference: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Document Processing

blocks.text: The automatic typesetting of academic papers has long been a challenge for researchers who prefer to focus on content rather than formatting. This paper demonstrates the minimal viable output of the IEEE Layout Compiler.

Subsequent paragraphs are indented according to the IEEE double-column A4 style guide. The compiler is designed to be deterministic: given the same input JSON, it always produces byte-identical output.
Many authors spend significant time reformatting papers for different IEEE venues. An automated compiler eliminates this burden.
We have presented a minimal working example of the IEEE Layout Compiler. Future milestones will add figure insertion, equation rendering, and AI-assisted structural parsing.
